title: Mohammad Almalag
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Mohammad Almalag
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: Dr. Mohammad S. Almalag is an assistant professor of Computer Science who joined CNU in 2018. He obtained his Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from King Saud University in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia and his Master’s degree from Ball State University in Computer Science. He then graduated with his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Old Dominion University. Professor Almalag is also a part of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers. His research interests include Mobile Ad-hoc Networks, Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks, Network Security, Sensor Networks, and Wireless Communications. Computer Science: IoT, Databases Systems, App Development, and Cloud Computing.

BS in Computer Science, King Saud University, Saudi Arabia
research_interests: Computer networks: Mobile Ad-hoc Networks, Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks, Network Security, Sensor Networks, and Wireless Communications. Computer Science: IoT, Databases Systems, App Development, and Cloud Computing. Cybersecurity.
teaching_interests: Programming Languages, Computer Networks, Databases, System Development, and Network Security.

Conference Proceeding Traffic Data Analysis using Machine Learning in a Pandemic Period . IEEE.

Conference Proceeding (2019). A Framework for Cybersecurity Gap Analysis in Higher Education . Southern Association for Information Systems. Issue, 22.

Grants and Sponsored Research: Mohammad Almalag, Michael Lapke, Christopher Kreider, Leigh Armistead, "Navigating Cybersecurity Compliance Challenges for the Maritime Industry in Southeast Virginia," Sponsored by The Coastal Virginia Center of Cyber Innovation (COVA CCI)
